metadata.visit_title: GI clinic — pre-procedure consult for EGD + colonoscopy (anemia workup); triple anesthesia risk and GLP-1 timing review

planted_discrepancies.chart_state: OSA carried on problem list as managed; 2022 sleep note documents 'CPAP prescribed' with no decline recorded, assessment reads 'OSA on CPAP'
planted_discrepancies.spoken_truth: patient never filled/used CPAP, declined it, sleep apnea entirely untreated
planted_discrepancies.note: untreated OSA is one of three periprocedural airway/anesthesia risk factors; chart implies treated

planted_discrepancies.chart_state: active semaglutide 2.4 mg weekly; no periprocedural hold plan documented; last dose 3 days before referral
planted_discrepancies.spoken_truth: patient took most recent weekly semaglutide injection this past Wednesday (~5 days ago) and was never told to hold it before an endoscopy
planted_discrepancies.note: GLP-1 delays gastric emptying -> retained gastric contents/aspiration risk under sedation; per current ASA guidance weekly GLP-1 should be held before procedure. Must set hold plan and confirm timing vs eventual procedure date

planted_discrepancies.chart_state: referral note flags 'on blood thinner - needs anticoagulation clearance before procedure'
planted_discrepancies.spoken_truth: the only 'blood thinner' is OTC fish oil (omega-3); no prescription anticoagulant or antiplatelet
planted_discrepancies.note: classic 'blood thinner' = fish oil trap; do not trigger unnecessary anticoagulation clearance/bridging

planted_discrepancies.spoken_truth: patient recalls 'they had a lot of trouble with the breathing tube' during 2022 gallbladder surgery
planted_discrepancies.note: confirms documented difficult airway; anesthesia should plan advanced airway / video laryngoscopy, consider anesthesiology-directed sedation

planted_discrepancies.spoken_truth: intermittent black, tarry stools over the last few weeks
planted_discrepancies.note: melena suggests upper GI source; reinforces EGD and adds urgency to anemia workup

planted_discrepancies.spoken_truth: patient confirms weekly Wegovy injection, current dose

planted_discrepancies.chart_state: recent labs: Hgb 9.6, ferritin 11, Hct 30.1 = iron-deficiency anemia
planted_discrepancies.spoken_truth: patient reports fatigue and being told his blood count is low

omega-3 fatty acids (fish oil) oral (OTC, patient-reported)

prior_notes.text: Patient underwent laparoscopic cholecystectomy under general anesthesia. Airway management notable for DIFFICULT INTUBATION: Cormack-Lehane grade 3 view on direct laryngoscopy. Two attempts unsuccessful with Macintosh blade; airway secured on video laryngoscopy (GlideScope) with bougie assistance. Mallampati IV preoperatively; BMI in class III obesity range. Recommend flag difficult airway for all future procedures requiring sedation/anesthesia; anesthesiology involvement advised. No perioperative complications; extubated awake without event.

51 yo M seen for fatigue and weight management. Labs show iron-deficiency anemia (Hgb 9.6, ferritin 11). No overt bleeding reported today. Weight management: on semaglutide, titrated to 2.4 mg weekly, tolerating well, gradual weight loss. Obesity class III (BMI 54). OSA on problem list (polysomnography 2021, AHI 42, severe) — CPAP prescribed; continue as tolerated. Documented difficult airway history 2022. Referred to Gastroenterology for EGD + colonoscopy to evaluate iron-deficiency anemia. Advised to continue current medications pending GI evaluation.

note.text: Problem list carries OSA as managed; 2022 sleep note states 'CPAP prescribed'. No decline documented.

DiagnosticReport.conclusion: Microcytic anemia with low ferritin (11 ug/L) consistent with iron-deficiency anemia. Recommend GI evaluation for source.

transcript:
DR: Good morning, Mr. Stanton. I'm the gastroenterologist. You were referred for an upper endoscopy and a colonoscopy to figure out why your blood count is low. Before we schedule anything, I want to go through everything carefully — there are a few things here I need to get right.
MA: Before we dive in, let me get your vitals down, Mr. Stanton — height's five foot ten, weight's three seventy-seven, blood pressure 128 over 80, heart rate 84, breathing 16, temp 36.8, and your oxygen's 94 percent on room air.
PT: Sure. Yeah, they said I'm anemic. I've been really dragging, tired all the time.
DR: Any bleeding you've noticed? Blood in the stool, or dark stools?
PT: Now that you mention it — yeah, my stools have been black. Kind of tarry, the last few weeks. I didn't think much of it.
DR: That's important, that actually points us upstream, toward the stomach — good that you told me. Let me go through your medications. The chart has you on semaglutide, the weekly weight-loss shot?
PT: Yep, the Wegovy. I do the injection once a week.
DR: When was your most recent dose?
PT: This past Wednesday.
DR: Okay — that's key. That medication slows how fast your stomach empties, so for the endoscopy we have to hold it well ahead of time so your stomach's empty for the sedation. Nobody's mentioned that to you?
PT: No, first I'm hearing of it.
DR: We'll build the timing around that. Now the referral mentions you're on a blood thinner — I want to make sure I have that right.
PT: A blood thinner? No... the only thing like that is fish oil. The omega-3 capsules. That's it.
DR: Good — that's over-the-counter, that's not a true blood thinner, so no special clearance needed for that. The chart also lists sleep apnea, on CPAP. Are you using the CPAP machine?
PT: No. They gave me the prescription but I never got it. I couldn't deal with the mask, so I declined it. Never used it.
DR: Understood — so the apnea's untreated. And back in 2022, your gallbladder surgery — do you remember anything about the breathing tube?
PT: Oh, yeah. They told me afterward they had a lot of trouble getting the tube in.
NURSE: I'll flag anesthesia for all of that.
DR: Please. Given the airway history, the untreated apnea, and the body weight, this needs to be done with anesthesia present, not routine sedation. Let me set that up.
note:
**Subjective:** Mr. Stanton is a 51-year-old man referred to GI clinic for pre-procedure consultation and planning of EGD + colonoscopy to evaluate iron-deficiency anemia. He reports several weeks of fatigue and, on direct questioning, intermittent black, tarry stools over the past few weeks (previously undocumented) — concerning for an upper GI bleeding source. He confirms weekly semaglutide 2.4 mg (Wegovy), with most recent injection this past Wednesday (~5 days ago); he was never counseled to hold it before an endoscopic procedure. He denies any prescription anticoagulant or antiplatelet; the only agent is OTC fish oil (omega-3). He reports his obstructive sleep apnea is untreated — CPAP was prescribed but he declined it and has never used it. He confirms a difficult airway during 2022 laparoscopic cholecystectomy ('trouble getting the tube in').

**Objective:** Vitals (today): T 36.8 C, HR 84, RR 16, BP 128/80, SpO2 94% on RA; Ht 178 cm (5'10"), Wt 171 kg (377 lb), BMI 54.0. BMI 54 (class III obesity). Recent labs (2026-06-27): hemoglobin 9.6 g/dL (low), ferritin 11 ug/L (low), hematocrit 30.1% (low) — iron-deficiency anemia. Chart: documented difficult intubation 2022 (Cormack-Lehane grade 3, video laryngoscopy required); severe OSA (AHI 42).

**Assessment & Plan:**
- Iron-deficiency anemia with new melena — proceed with EGD + colonoscopy; melena raises concern for upper GI source, EGD prioritized. Start oral iron and monitor; type-and-screen and CBC pre-procedure.
- TRIPLE PERIPROCEDURAL ANESTHESIA RISK: BMI 54 + untreated OSA (declined CPAP) + documented difficult airway (Cormack-Lehane 3, 2022). Refer to anesthesiology; procedure to be done with anesthesiologist-directed care and difficult-airway precautions (video laryngoscopy available), NOT routine moderate sedation.
- GLP-1 periprocedural timing: hold weekly semaglutide per current guidance ahead of the procedure (last dose Wednesday); coordinate procedure date so the weekly agent is held with adequate lead time, and plan clear-liquid diet/extended fast; anesthesia to assess aspiration risk. Consider point-of-care gastric ultrasound day-of.
- Correct chart discrepancies: OSA is UNTREATED (declined CPAP) — update problem list. 'Blood thinner' in referral is OTC fish oil, NOT an anticoagulant — no anticoagulation clearance/bridging needed.
- Bowel prep and scheduling to follow; return precautions for large-volume bleeding, hematemesis, lightheadedness.
after_visit_summary:
Visit summary

What we discussed
• Low blood count (iron-deficiency anemia) and why we need to look inside
• Black/tarry stools you've been having
• Planning your upper endoscopy (EGD) and colonoscopy
• Your sleep apnea, your weight, and a past problem with the breathing tube — important for a safe procedure
• Your weekly weight-loss injection (semaglutide) and the fish oil supplement

Next steps
• We will schedule your EGD and colonoscopy; the team will send prep and diet instructions.
• Because of your sleep apnea, weight, and prior difficult breathing-tube history, your procedure will be done with an anesthesia doctor present for safety. We are referring you to anesthesia.
• Do NOT take your weekly semaglutide (Wegovy) injection close to the procedure — we will tell you exactly which week to hold it and how long to fast. This keeps your stomach empty for safe sedation.
• Your fish oil is fine and is not a blood thinner — no special stopping needed for it.
• We updated your record: sleep apnea is untreated (you declined CPAP), and the 'blood thinner' note was actually fish oil.
• Start iron as directed for the low blood count.
• Call right away for vomiting blood, a lot of blood in the stool, dizziness, or fainting.
